description: The Nuova Famiglia was an Italian Camorra confederation created in the late 1970s to oppose Raffaele Cutolo's Nuova Camorra Organizzata. It was headed by powerful Camorra bosses including Carmine Alfieri, the Nuvoletta brothers, Michele Zaza, Luigi Giuliano, and Antonio Bardellino. The organization was active primarily in Campania and had ties with the Sicilian Mafia. It engaged in various criminal activities such as drug trafficking, murder, and extortion until its dissolution in the 1990s.

country_links.evidence_quote: Antonio Bardellino was murdered by his right-hand man, Mario Iovine in his Brazilian home at Búzios
country_links.context: The organization's boss Antonio Bardellino was murdered in his Brazilian home, indicating the organization had a presence there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Territory: All Campania and the cities of Rome , Ostia , Milan and Rimini in Italy.
country_links.context: The organization operates in multiple Italian cities including Rome, Ostia, Milan, and Rimini.
